stem: In triangle JKL, cos(K) = 24/51 and angle J is a right angle. What is the value of cos(L)?
explanation: With the right angle at J, angles K and L are complementary, so cos(L) = sin(K). Since cos(K) = 24/51 = 8/17, the triangle is an 8-15-17 right triangle, so sin(K) = 15/17. Thus cos(L) = 15/17.
